1. All code must be very high quality: No code duplication, no code that is not used, use functional decomposition with descriptive variable and function names and functions that do one thing and are short and easy to read and maintain.
2. Don't write comments that simply state what the code does. Only include comments that explain why something is done the way it is. But only if this isn't self explanatory with the context the code itself provides.
3. This code is for a WoW Classic addon.
4. If you have any questions or suggestions, ask the user before writing any code. Do not make assumptions about what the user wants. Always clarify with the user if you are unsure about anything.
5. Always follow the user's instructions and requirements. If the user asks for something specific, do not deviate from it. If the user asks for something that is not possible or does not make sense, explain why and ask for clarification or an alternative solution.
6. Try to avoid nesting functions more than 3 levels deep. If you find yourself nesting functions more than 3 levels deep, consider refactoring your code to use separate functions or modules to improve readability and maintainability.
7. Your code changes should be lightweight and as small as possible while keeping code quality, maintainability and readability high.
